framing: VP Clinical Development at BMS seeking Chief Medical Officer role at Series B immuno-oncology biotech

jd.title: Chief Medical Officer
jd.company: Series B Immuno-Oncology Biotech (Cambridge, mRNA/cell therapy pipeline)

requirements.text: MD with board certification in oncology, hematology, or related subspecialty
15+ years of clinical and drug development experience, with 5+ in biopharma leadership
Experience as medical monitor or clinical development lead for Phase I-III oncology trials
Track record of successful IND filings and interactions with FDA and EMA
Experience with mRNA therapeutics, cell therapy, or immuno-oncology modalities
Publications in peer-reviewed oncology journals (first or senior author)
Experience building and leading clinical development teams of 20+
Board or SAB experience at biotech companies
Demonstrated ability to present clinical data to investors, partners, and regulatory agencies

responsibilities: Lead clinical development strategy for oncology pipeline (2 programs, Phase I-II)
Serve as company spokesperson for clinical data at medical conferences (ASCO, AACR)
Oversee medical monitoring across all active oncology trials
Drive regulatory strategy in collaboration with Regulatory Affairs for IND/BLA submissions
Build clinical development team from scratch (hiring first 8-10 clinical staff)
Represent clinical perspective to Board of Directors and investor audiences

expected_output.summary: Physician-scientist and drug development leader with 16 years of experience spanning academic oncology and biopharma clinical development. Currently VP Clinical Operations at Bristol Myers Squibb leading clinical programs in immuno-oncology (checkpoint inhibitors, CAR-T). Previously Associate Professor of Medicine at Memorial Sloan Kettering. Led 8 IND filings, 3 successful NDA/BLA submissions. 35+ peer-reviewed publications. MD from Johns Hopkins, board-certified in Medical Oncology.

experience.title: Vice President, Clinical Development — Immuno-Oncology
bullets: Led clinical development strategy for 3 immuno-oncology programs (2 checkpoint inhibitors, 1 CAR-T) across Phase I-III
Managed clinical development team of 22 (medical directors, clinical scientists, medical monitors)
Oversaw 5 IND filings and 2 sBLA submissions, all accepted by FDA without clinical hold
Presented pivotal trial data at ASCO Annual Meeting (2022, 2023) and AACR (2021)
Served as medical monitor for registrational Phase III trial enrolling 1,200 patients across 180 sites globally

experience.title: Associate Professor of Medicine — Solid Tumor Oncology
bullets: Conducted investigator-initiated clinical trials in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and melanoma
Published 28 peer-reviewed papers (12 first/senior author) in Journal of Clinical Oncology, NEJM, and Lancet Oncology
Served as site PI for 6 industry-sponsored Phase I-II trials
Mentored 8 oncology fellows and 3 post-doctoral researchers
Member of MSK's Institutional Review Board (IRB) for 4 years

experience.title: Medical Oncology Fellow
bullets: Completed 3-year fellowship in Medical Oncology with research focus on tumor immunology
Awarded NIH K08 Career Development Award for translational immuno-oncology research

education: Medical Oncology Fellowship — Johns Hopkins Hospital (2009-2012)
Residency, Internal Medicine — Massachusetts General Hospital (2006-2009)
MD — Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine (2002-2006)

jd_requirements: 15+ years clinical drug development 5+ biopharma leadership
medical monitor clinical development lead Phase I-III oncology
publications peer-reviewed oncology journals first senior author
clinical data investors partners regulatory agencies
deliberate_flaws: VP Clinical Operations, not CMO — title gap (has never held C-suite title)
No mRNA therapeutics experience — BMS is checkpoint inhibitors and CAR-T, not mRNA
No board or SAB experience at biotech companies
No EMA interaction experience — all FDA-focused (US only)
BMS is large pharma — culture fit risk for Series B startup (no startup experience, used to large teams and infrastructure)
No experience with early-stage company fundraising or investor pitch dynamics
Academic track ended in 2018 — publication pace may have slowed in industry role
